title: Wooden Squatty Potty
summary: A simple three-board footstool platform — a top, two side supports, and a front brace — sized to sit in front of the toilet.
description: This is about as few parts as a stable stool can have: one top board carried by two side supports and a single front brace tying them together, with no back needed since the unit sits flush against the base of the toilet in use. The side-support height is the one number worth treating as a starting point rather than a fixed rule — cut them taller or shorter than the stock 8-1/4" to match your own toilet height and the actual squat angle you're building for.

cutList.note: Adjust taller or shorter for a different stool height.

steps.title: Cut the pieces
steps.body: Cut the top, two sides, and front support to size.

steps.title: Attach the sides to the top
steps.body: Drill pocket holes in the top edges of the two side pieces and attach them to the underside of the top board, positioned near the front and back edges so the top is fully supported.

steps.title: Attach the front support
steps.body: Drill pocket holes in the ends of the front support and attach it between the two sides at the front, tying the whole stool together.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand every surface smooth, easing any sharp edges, and apply a moisture-resistant finish suited to bathroom use.
